1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */
16	
17	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
18	import type { Exercise } from '@/payload-types'
19	import type { ContentBlock } from '@/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas'
20	import type { DuplicationSubject } from '@/server/payload/collections/LessonDuplications'
21	import { logger } from '@/infra/utils/logger'
22	import {
23	  selectExercisesScaled,
24	  selectSectionsForVariation,
25	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/selectors'
26	import { getSourceExercisesForLesson } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/source-exercises'
27	import {
28	  validateExerciseStructural,
29	  fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders,
30	  BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES,
31	  type StructuralFailure,
32	  type FailureCode,
33	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/structural'
34	import {
35	  validateExerciseSemantic,
36	  SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
37	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/semantic'
38	import { RouterStrategy } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/strategies/router'
39	
40	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
41	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
42	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
43	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
44	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
45	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
46	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
47	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches
48	// any bump (current value preserved as literal `1`).
49	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
50	
51	export const GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE = 'GENERATION_FAILED' as const
52	export const STUCK_FAILURE_CODE = 'STUCK_AFTER_MAX_ATTEMPTS' as const
53	
54	export type DuplicationStrategy = 'script' | 'ai'
55	
56	/** Result of running the strategy for one exercise. */
57	export interface StrategyResult {
58	  exerciseId: string
59	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
60	  blocks: ContentBlock[]
61	}
62	
63	/**
64	 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
65	 *
66	 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
67	 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
68	 *
69	 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
70	 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
71	 * @param _payload    Payload instance (available for future AI strategy use)
72	 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
73	 */
74	export async function runStrategy(
75	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
76	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
77	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
78	  payload: Payload,
79	): Promise<StrategyResult & { tokensUsed: { inputTokens: number; outputTokens: number } }> {
80	  const router = new RouterStrategy(payload)
81	  const result = await router.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)
82	
83	  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
84	  return {
85	    exerciseId: exercise.id,
86	    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',
87	    blocks: content.blocks ?? [],
88	    tokensUsed: result.tokensUsed ?? { inputTokens: 0, outputTokens: 0 },
89	  }
90	}
91	
92	/**
93	 * Cap source-exercise block count to match the structural validator's
94	 * `TOO_MANY_SECTIONS` ceiling (5). Pure: returns a NEW exercise object with
95	 * a trimmed `content.blocks` array when needed, otherwise the original.
96	 *
97	 * Picks 5 representative blocks from the source via `selectSectionsScaled` —
98	 * the same scaling-random bucketing the lesson-level selector uses. Preserves
99	 * source order, so the variation reads like a natural shorter version of the
100	 * original rather than a random shuffle.
101	 *
102	 * Rationale: without this, a 10-block source always failed validation
103	 * (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS is a blocking failure → exercise dropped from output).
104	 * Admins doing deep variations on real lessons would silently lose any
105	 * exercise that exceeded the block cap.
106	 */
107	export function trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise: ExerciseDoc): ExerciseDoc {
108	  const blocks = exercise.content?.blocks
109	  if (!Array.isArray(blocks) || blocks.length <= 5) return exercise
110	  const picked = selectSectionsForVariation(blocks, 5)
111	  logger.info(
112	    { exerciseId: exercise.id, originalCount: blocks.length, trimmedTo: picked.length },
113	    'Source exercise has more than 5 blocks — trimming via variation-aware selector',
114	  )
115	  return {
116	    ...exercise,
117	    content: { ...exercise.content, blocks: picked },
118	  }
119	}

121	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
122	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
123	  switch (code) {
124	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
125	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
126	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
127	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
128	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
129	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
130	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
131	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
132	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
133	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
134	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
135	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
136	      return 'regenerate'
137	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
138	      return 'regenerate'
139	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
140	      return 'skip'
141	    default:
142	      return 'skip'
143	  }
144	}

146	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
147	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
148	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
149	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
150	// before deleting this assert.
151	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
152	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true
153	void _concurrencyAssert

155	/**
156	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
157	 *
158	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
159	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
160	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
161	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
162	 */
163	async function appendEntry(
164	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
165	  payload: Payload,
166	  duplicationId: string,
167	  exerciseRef: string,
168	  sectionIndex: number,
169	  code: string,
170	  message: string,
171	): Promise<void> {
172	  const action = suggestAction(code)
173	  try {
174	    const current = await payload.findByID({
175	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
176	      id: duplicationId,
177	      depth: 0,
178	      overrideAccess: true,
179	    })
180	    await payload.update({
181	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
182	      id: duplicationId,
183	      data: {
184	        [bucket]: [
185	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
186	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
187	          {
188	            exerciseRef,
189	            sectionIndex,
190	            code,
191	            message,
192	            suggestedAction: action,
193	            resolved: false,
194	          },
195	        ],
196	      } as never,
197	      overrideAccess: true,
198	    })
199	  } catch (err) {
200	    logger.error(
201	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
202	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
203	    )
204	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
205	  }
206	}

208	/** Append a blocking failure (exercise dropped from output lesson). */
209	export async function appendFailure(
210	  payload: Payload,
211	  duplicationId: string,
212	  exerciseRef: string,
213	  sectionIndex: number,
214	  code: string,
215	  message: string,
216	): Promise<void> {
217	  return appendEntry('failures', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
218	}

220	/** Append a non-blocking warning (exercise kept with placeholder). */
221	export async function appendWarning(
222	  payload: Payload,
223	  duplicationId: string,
224	  exerciseRef: string,
225	  sectionIndex: number,
226	  code: string,

232	/**
233	 * Append a successful output-exercise mapping to the LessonDuplications
234	 * record. Used to stream progress so a Vercel function timeout doesn't lose
235	 * work that's already in the DB. Read-modify-write on the record's
236	 * `outputExercises` array — safe under CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1.
237	 */
238	async function appendOutputExercise(
239	  payload: Payload,
240	  duplicationId: string,
241	  mapping: OutputExerciseMapping,
242	): Promise<void> {

252	    await payload.update({

261	      'Failed to append output exercise mapping',

263	  }
264	}

266	/** Shape of an exercise from the exercises collection. */
267	export type ExerciseDoc = {

519	/**
520	 * Reserve at least this much wall time (ms) at the end of a tick so the
521	 * orchestrator can write its final state to the DB before the platform
522	 * (Vercel) yanks the function. 30s covers a slow Mongo round-trip plus
523	 * Vercel's teardown grace.
524	 */
525	const FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS = 30_000
526	
527	/**
528	 * Default assumed worst-case time for one exercise (pass-1 + pass-2 +
529	 * validators on gemini-3.1-pro-preview). Used to decide whether the next
530	 * exercise can fit before the deadline. Conservative — if the run is
531	 * faster, we just process more per tick.
532	 */
533	const ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS = 300_000
534	
535	export type RunOutcome = 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' | 'failed' | 'in_progress'
536	
537	export interface RunOptions {
538	  /**
539	   * Absolute wall-clock deadline (Date.now() ms). The orchestrator returns
540	   * `in_progress` as soon as the remaining time is too short to fit another
541	   * exercise — leaving the record in `running` so the next cron tick picks
542	   * up where this one left off. Default: no deadline (process all).
543	   */
544	  deadlineMs?: number
545	}

547	/**
548	 * Orchestrate the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record.
549	 * Resumable: skips exercises already processed in previous ticks and streams
550	 * each new output mapping to the DB as it completes. A Vercel function
551	 * timeout mid-run leaves a clean partial state — the next cron tick continues
552	 * from there.
553	 *
554	 * @param duplicationId  ID of the LessonDuplications record to process
555	 * @param payload        Payload instance
556	 * @param options.deadlineMs  Absolute timestamp by which to stop processing
557	 *
558	 * Pre-condition: record has status `pending` (fresh) or `running` (resuming).
559	 * Post-condition (returned outcome):
560	 *  - 'succeeded'   — all picked exercises produced an output, 0 failures
561	 *  - 'needs_review'— all picked exercises terminal, ≥1 failure
562	 *  - 'in_progress' — deadline hit, more work to do, status left as 'running'
563	 *  - 'failed'      — fatal error before any exercises processed
564	 */
565	export async function runDuplicationOrchestrator(
566	  duplicationId: string,
567	  payload: Payload,
568	  options: RunOptions = {},
569	): Promise<RunOutcome> {
570	  // Load the duplication record
571	  const duplication = await payload.findByID({
572	    collection: 'lesson-duplications',
573	    id: duplicationId,
574	    depth: 1, // need sourceLesson relationship
575	    overrideAccess: true,
576	  })
577	
578	  if (!duplication) {
579	    logger.error({ duplicationId }, 'LessonDuplications record not found')
580	    return 'failed'
581	  }
582	
583	  // Already terminal — nothing to do.
584	  if (
585	    duplication.status === 'succeeded' ||
586	    duplication.status === 'needs_review' ||
587	    duplication.status === 'failed'
588	  ) {
589	    logger.warn(
590	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
591	      'LessonDuplications record already terminal, skipping',
592	    )
593	    return duplication.status as RunOutcome
594	  }
595	
596	  // Accept both 'pending' (first tick) and 'running' (resuming) — anything
597	  // else is unexpected.
598	  if (duplication.status !== 'pending' && duplication.status !== 'running') {
599	    logger.warn(
600	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
601	      'LessonDuplications record in unexpected status, skipping',
602	    )
603	    return 'failed'
604	  }
605	
606	  try {
607	    const sourceLessonId =
608	      typeof duplication.sourceLesson === 'string'
609	        ? duplication.sourceLesson
610	        : (duplication.sourceLesson as { id?: string })?.id
611	
612	    if (!sourceLessonId) {
613	      throw new Error('sourceLesson relationship is missing or invalid')
614	    }
615	
616	    // First tick: create output lesson, flip to running. Resuming tick:
617	    // reuse the existing output lesson recorded on the record.
618	    let outputLessonId: string
619	    const existingOutputLessonRef = duplication.outputLesson as
620	      | string
621	      | { id?: string }
622	      | null
623	      | undefined
624	    const existingOutputLessonId =
625	      typeof existingOutputLessonRef === 'string'
626	        ? existingOutputLessonRef
627	        : existingOutputLessonRef?.id
628	
629	    if (duplication.status === 'pending') {
630	      outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
631	        payload,
632	        sourceLessonId,
633	        duplication.level ?? 'light',
634	      )
635	      await payload.update({
636	        collection: 'lesson-duplications',
637	        id: duplicationId,
638	        data: { status: 'running', outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
639	        overrideAccess: true,
640	      })
641	    } else {
642	      // resuming
643	      if (!existingOutputLessonId) {
644	        // Shouldn't happen — running record without an outputLesson means a
645	        // previous tick crashed before the initial write. Treat as fresh.
646	        outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
647	          payload,
648	          sourceLessonId,
649	          duplication.level ?? 'light',
650	        )
651	        await payload.update({
652	          collection: 'lesson-duplications',
653	          id: duplicationId,
654	          data: { outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
655	          overrideAccess: true,
656	        })
657	      } else {
658	        outputLessonId = existingOutputLessonId
659	      }
660	    }
661	
662	    const allExercises = await getSourceExercisesForLesson(payload, sourceLessonId)
663	
664	    // No artificial cap — resumability handles long runs across multiple
665	    // cron ticks. selectExercisesScaled still bounds at 20 per the original
666	    // product spec (admin scaling-random across the whole lesson).
667	    const PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP = 20
668	    const selectedExercises = selectExercisesScaled(
669	      allExercises as ExerciseDoc[],
670	      PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP,
671	    )
672	
673	    // Filter out exercises already processed on prior ticks. Sources of truth:
674	    //  1. The record's `outputExercises` — explicitly recorded successes.
675	    //  2. The record's `failures` — terminal per-exercise failures.
676	    //  3. Output exercises already present in the output lesson itself, even
677	    //     if their mapping never made it back into `outputExercises`. This
678	    //     closes the crash-window between createOutputExercise (writes the
679	    //     exercise to Mongo) and appendOutputExercise (records the mapping):
680	    //     if the function dies between those two calls, the exercise is
681	    //     durably saved but the record doesn't know about it; without this
682	    //     check the next tick would re-process the source and create a
683	    //     duplicate. We identify the source by the deterministic title
684	    //     `Variation of <sourceExerciseId>` set in createOutputExercise.
685	    const existingOutputExercises = await payload.find({
686	      collection: 'exercises',
687	      where: { lesson: { equals: outputLessonId } },
688	      limit: 0,
689	      depth: 0,
690	      overrideAccess: true,
691	    })
692	    const titleSourceIds = new Set<string>()
693	    const orphanMappings: OutputExerciseMapping[] = []
694	    const knownMappingSources = new Set(
695	      ((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
696	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
697	      ),
698	    )
699	    for (const ex of existingOutputExercises.docs as Array<{
700	      id: string
701	      title?: string
702	    }>) {
703	      const match = typeof ex.title === 'string' ? /^Variation of (\S+)/.exec(ex.title) : null
704	      if (match) {
705	        titleSourceIds.add(match[1])
706	        // Recover the mapping for any orphan exercise (created but never
707	        // recorded). Next tick start, we'll reconcile by appending these
708	        // mappings so the record matches reality going forward.
709	        if (!knownMappingSources.has(match[1])) {
710	          orphanMappings.push({
711	            sourceExerciseId: match[1],
712	            outputExerciseId: ex.id,
713	            strategy: 'ai',
714	          })
715	        }
716	      }
717	    }
718	
719	    // Reconcile orphan mappings recovered from the output lesson so the
720	    // duplications record reflects reality before this tick continues.
721	    for (const orphan of orphanMappings) {
722	      await appendOutputExercise(payload, duplicationId, orphan)
723	      logger.warn(
724	        { duplicationId, sourceExerciseId: orphan.sourceExerciseId },
725	        '[orchestrator] Recovered orphan output exercise (crash-window mapping)',
726	      )
727	    }
728	
729	    const processedIds = new Set<string>([
730	      ...((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
731	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
732	      ),
733	      ...((duplication.failures as unknown as Array<{ exerciseRef: string }>) ?? []).map(
734	        (f) => f.exerciseRef,
735	      ),
736	      ...titleSourceIds,
737	    ])
738	    const remaining = selectedExercises.filter((ex) => !processedIds.has(ex.id))
739	
740	    logger.info(
741	      {
742	        duplicationId,
743	        totalPicked: selectedExercises.length,
744	        alreadyProcessed: selectedExercises.length - remaining.length,
745	        remaining: remaining.length,
746	      },
747	      'Orchestrator tick start',
748	    )
749	
750	    const duplicationLevel = duplication.level as 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep'
751	    const duplicationSubject =
752	      (duplication.subject as DuplicationSubject | null | undefined) ?? 'mixed'
753	
754	    // Process exercises one at a time (CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 — appendEntry's
755	    // read-modify-write isn't safe under parallel writes; see compile-time
756	    // guard). Check deadline between exercises and bail out if the next one
757	    // can't fit, returning `in_progress` so the next tick continues.
758	    for (const exercise of remaining) {
759	      if (options.deadlineMs !== undefined) {
760	        const remainingMs = options.deadlineMs - Date.now()
761	        if (remainingMs < FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS + ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS) {
762	          logger.info(
763	            { duplicationId, remainingMs, remainingExercises: remaining.length },
764	            'Orchestrator tick yielding before deadline — leaving record running',
765	          )
766	          return 'in_progress'
767	        }
768	      }
769	
770	      const { result } = await processExercise(
771	        exercise,
772	        duplicationId,
773	        duplicationLevel,
774	        duplicationSubject,
775	        payload,
776	      )
777	
778	      if (result === null) continue
779	
780	      try {
781	        const mapping = await createOutputExercise(payload, result, outputLessonId)
782	        // Stream the success immediately so a function timeout doesn't lose
783	        // this exercise — the actual exercise doc is already in MongoDB
784	        // (createOutputExercise just created it), this just keeps the
785	        // duplications record in sync.
786	        await appendOutputExercise(payload, duplicationId, mapping)
787	      } catch (err) {
788	        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown create error'
789	        logger.error(
790	          { exerciseRef: exercise.id, err },
791	          'createOutputExercise failed — exercise dropped from output lesson',
792	        )
793	        await appendFailure(
794	          payload,
795	          duplicationId,
796	          exercise.id,
797	          0,
798	          GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE,
799	          `Failed to save variation: ${message}`,
800	        )
801	      }
802	    }
803	
804	    // All selected exercises terminal: finalize status. Re-read failures
805	    // count from the record since we streamed entries during the run.
806	    const finalRecord = await payload.findByID({
807	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
808	      id: duplicationId,
809	      depth: 0,
810	      overrideAccess: true,
811	    })
812	    const finalFailures = (finalRecord.failures as unknown as unknown[] | undefined)?.length ?? 0
813	    const finalOutputs =
814	      (finalRecord.outputExercises as unknown as unknown[] | undefined)?.length ?? 0
815	    const finalStatus: 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' =
816	      finalFailures === 0 ? 'succeeded' : 'needs_review'
817	
818	    logger.info(
819	      {
820	        duplicationId,
821	        total: selectedExercises.length,
822	        succeeded: finalOutputs,
823	        failed: finalFailures,
824	        finalStatus,
825	      },
826	      'Duplication orchestrator completed',
827	    )
828	
829	    await payload.update({
830	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
831	      id: duplicationId,
832	      data: {
833	        status: finalStatus,
834	      } as never,
835	      overrideAccess: true,
836	    })
837	    return finalStatus
838	  } catch (err) {
839	    logger.error({ duplicationId, err }, 'Orchestrator run failed')
840	    await payload.update({
841	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
842	      id: duplicationId,
843	      data: { status: 'failed' },
844	      overrideAccess: true,
845	    })
846	    throw err
847	  }
848	}
